title: Agility Robotics rejects AI and robotics talent 'bidding wars'
excerpt: Agility Robotics has expanded with a new Silicon Valley hub. The startup is avoiding AI talent wars and focusing on culture over high salaries.
content_html:
Humanoid startup Agility Robotics doesn't want 'coin-operated employees'

By Lloyd Lee

You're currently following this author! Want to unfollow? Unsubscribe via the link in your email.

Agility Robotics has a new hub in Silicon Valley, where it will be luring more world-class engineers to join the humanoid maker. Just don't expect the startup to get into a spending war for talent.

Jonathan Hurst, Agility's cofounder and chief robot officer, told Business Insider his company has no interest in joining the escalating competition over pay packages for top AI and robotics talent.

"One of the things that's been weird recently has been kind of the bidding wars for some of this really rare talent," Hurst said. "We're not going to do that because we don't want coin-operated employees."

On Thursday, Agility opened a 60,000-square-foot research and training facility in Fremont, where the company expects to house nearly 200 existing and new employees across hardware engineering, AI and machine learning, and customer operations support.

The site will serve as a "robot school" to teach Agility's Digit humanoid new skills, Hurst said.

The Fremont location, about 15 miles east of Palo Alto, gives Agility a closer proximity to one of the world's deepest pools of technical talent. It also means Agility will be near some of the most well-funded companies competing in the robotics space.

On the other side of the road is Meta's own Fremont campus, which houses the company's hardware R&D team, including its robotics outfit. About 10 miles south is Tesla's vast Fremont center, where the company is preparing for Optimus production. And across the Dumbarton Bridge in Palo Alto is a cluster of other robotics companies, including 1X, Mind Robotics, and Serve Robotics.

Competition for top AI talent has been fierce, at times leading to eye-popping compensation packages. At Meta, top hires have been offered nine-figure signing bonuses. Other AI leaders at smaller outfits can see between $300,000 and $400,000 in base pay, Business Insider previously reported.

Hurst said he has seen candidates offered what Agility believes is a fair, market-rate offer, only for the prospective hire to come back and say they've received double from another company.

"We're like, 'Go for it.' That's okay," Hurst said, adding that the startup prefers candidates who fit Agility's culture and mission.

To lure top talent, Hurst points to his company's progress: "We're the first to commercially deploy humanoid robots."

Agility is one of the few robotics startups in the US with humanoids performing sustained work beyond the confines of a research lab.

The company first deployed Digit, a bipedal robot, for commercial operations in 2024. Agility has since deployed the bot across manufacturing, distribution, and logistics operations, accumulating more than 65,000 hours of work.

Digit's primary tasks for now have been picking up bins and moving them to another location, Hurst said.

"We're doing this in a serious way. The we-are-going-to-build-a-sustainable-growing-company way," Hurst said. "It's not so much a tell-a-grand-story-and-hope-we-can-fill-in-the-gaps way."

Agility plans to go public via a SPAC later this year. The startup said in an SEC filing that it has more than $300 million in conditional multiyear orders for its next-generation Digit V5.

Read next

Lloyd Lee

blocks.html_en: • Robotics

sentences.en: Robotics
sentences.zh: 机器人技术

content_html_zh:
人形初创公司 Agility Robotics 不想要“投币员工”

作者：Lloyd Lee 您目前正在关注该作者！ 想要取消关注吗？ 通过电子邮件中的链接取消订阅。

Agility Robotics 在硅谷设立了一个新中心，将吸引更多世界一流的工程师加入这家人形机器人制造商。 只是不要指望这家初创公司会陷入人才争夺战。

Agility 联合创始人兼首席机器人官乔纳森·赫斯特 (Jonathan Hurst) 告诉 Business Insider，他的公司没有兴趣加入针对顶级人工智能和机器人人才的薪酬方案不断升级的竞争。

“最近奇怪的事情之一就是对一些真正稀有人才的竞购战，”赫斯特说。 “我们不会这样做，因为我们不想要投币式员工。”

周四，Agility 在弗里蒙特开设了一个 60,000 平方英尺的研究和培训设施，该公司预计将容纳近 200 名现有和新员工，涵盖硬件工程、人工智能和机器学习以及客户运营支持。

赫斯特说，该网站将作为“机器人学校”来教授 Agility 的数字人形机器人新技能。

弗里蒙特位于帕洛阿尔托以东约 15 英里处，使 Agility 更接近世界上最深厚的技术人才库之一。 这也意味着Agility将接近一些在机器人领域竞争的资金最雄厚的公司。

路的另一边是 Meta 自己的弗里蒙特园区，这里设有该公司的硬件研发团队，包括其机器人设备。 向南约 10 英里是特斯拉庞大的弗里蒙特中心，该公司正在那里准备 Optimus 的生产。 帕洛阿尔托的敦巴顿桥对面是其他机器人公司的集群，包括 1X、Mind Robotics 和 Serve Robotics。

顶尖人工智能人才的竞争一直很激烈，有时会导致令人瞠目结舌的薪酬待遇。 在 Meta，顶级员工可以获得九位数的签约奖金。 据 Business Insider 此前报道，其他小型公司的人工智能领导者的基本工资在 30 万至 40 万美元之间。

Hurst 表示，他曾见过应聘者提供了 Agility 认为公平的市场价格报价，但潜在员工回来后却说他们从另一家公司收到了双倍的薪水。

“我们会说，‘放手去做吧。’ 没关系，”赫斯特说，并补充说，这家初创公司更喜欢符合 Agility 文化和使命的候选人。

为了吸引顶尖人才，赫斯特指出了他公司的进展：“我们是第一家商业部署人形机器人的公司。”

Agility 是美国为数不多的机器人初创公司之一，其类人机器人能够在研究实验室的范围之外进行持续的工作。

该公司于 2024 年首次部署双足机器人 Digit 进行商业运营。 此后，Agility 已将该机器人部署到制造、分销和物流运营领域，累计工作时间超过 65,000 小时。

赫斯特说，数字目前的主要任务是捡起垃圾箱并将其移至另一个位置。

“我们正在认真地做这件事。 我们将建立一个可持续发展的公司的方式，”赫斯特说。 “这并不是一种讲述宏大故事并希望我们能够填补空白的方式。”

Agility 计划在今年晚些时候通过 SPAC 上市。 该初创公司在 SEC 提交的文件中表示，其下一代 Digit V5 拥有价值超过 3 亿美元的有条件多年期订单。
